cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why it was one of the author's happiest times. The passage starts by saying, "It was one of the happiest times of my life. I was 29 and had just received my bachelor's degree, graduating with honors despite working two jobs and being a wife and mother." So the key points here are that she received her bachelor's degree with honors, even though she was working two jobs and being a wife and mother. The answer is probably option B because it directly mentions receiving her bachelor's degree. Looking at the choices: A) she became a good wife and got two jobs. Well, the passage mentions she was a wife and had two jobs, but the main point is that she got her degree despite those responsibilities, not that she became a good wife and got two jobs. So maybe not A. B) she had just received her bachelor's degree. Yes, the first sentence mentions receiving the degree as part of the happy time. So B is a strong candidate. C) she had been a teacher at Ashland University. No, the text says she was graduating from Ashland University, not that she was a teacher there. She later became a kindergarten teacher, but not at Ashland. D) she was proud to be loved by her family. The passage
